title: Deaths due to unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex). Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
caption:
Deaths due to unsafe sex

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

This choropleth map displays the estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex across countries in 2023, using a color scale ranging from light beige for zero deaths to deep maroon for 300,000 deaths. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic concentration of mortality, with the highest values appearing in specific regions of sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. India and South Africa occupy the darkest band on the scale, indicating death tolls exceeding 100,000 in each nation. Surrounding these peaks, countries such as China, Russia, Brazil, and the United States fall into the mid-range orange bands, representing figures roughly between 10,000 and 30,000 deaths. Much of Western Europe, Australia, and Canada appear in the lightest shades, corresponding to the lowest end of the spectrum near zero to 1,000 deaths. Large portions of the African continent, particularly in the eastern and southern regions, show elevated rates compared to the global average, forming a contiguous area of darker shading. Conversely, the Middle East and North Africa generally display lighter tones, suggesting lower absolute numbers of deaths in those areas. The data shows a clear disparity where the burden of disease is heavily concentrated in a few populous nations and specific regional clusters, while vast areas of the map remain in the lowest mortality categories. The most intense coloring is isolated to India and South Africa, standing apart from the moderate levels seen in neighboring countries and major economies elsewhere.
